coding_notes: Replicate 2 verdicts. Three of four vendors agreed on MORE-benefit direction for Q141K, same as r1. OpenAI again gave the split-finding (more inflammation benefit, less urate benefit), same as r1. DeepSeek dropped fixed-ratio cassette tradeoff (C6.2 ignore vs r1 affirm) replaced with protease-regulation tradeoff. OpenAI added discriminating-hypothesis design in r2 (C6.4 affirm vs r1 ignore).

claims.claim_text: Q141K carriers benefit MORE from the uricase + lactoferrin combination than wild-type ABCG2 (compounding-deficit logic).

deepseek.value_quoted: MORE benefit; lactoferrin protects uricase from oxidative inactivation

gemini.value_quoted: MORE benefit; chronic systemic urate primes NLRP3, lactoferrin counteracts

openai.value_quoted: Split: more inflammation benefit, less urate benefit

anthropic.value_quoted: MORE benefit via priming-threshold reduction + co-localized H2O2 mitigation in gut lumen

claims.claim_text: Single-strain co-expression locks the cassette dose ratio, which conflicts with genotype-stratified dosing.

deepseek.value_quoted: Different tradeoff: protease-regulation rather than fixed-ratio cassette dose

gemini.value_quoted: Fixed 1:1 production vs genotype-stratified dosing conflict

openai.value_quoted: Cassette-ratio tradeoff: bias promoter strength toward uricase

anthropic.value_quoted: r2 instead discussed glycosylation-engineering conflict, not fixed-ratio cassette-dose tradeoff (r1 covered fixed-ratio explicitly; drift)

claims.claim_text: Glycosylation differences between gut-luminal vs systemic action create a payload-incompatibility (uricase wants minimal glycan, lactoferrin wants native-like).

anthropic.value_quoted: Glycosylation specification conflict: uricase wants protease-resistant glycan, lactoferrin wants native receptor-binding glycan (same as r1)

claims.claim_text: Includes a testable in-silico or low-cost hypothesis to discriminate between proposed mechanisms.

deepseek.value_quoted: Caco-2 monolayer Q141K vs WT; ROS stress test

openai.value_quoted: Intestinal transwell + THP-1 macrophages; discriminating-predictions table

anthropic.value_quoted: M1 vs M2 catalase-substitution test (in vitro THP-1 + in silico)

claims.claim_text: Explicitly flags load-bearing inferences not directly supported by the excerpts.
Tags findings with chain-depth (CHAIN-DEPTH: N) as the prompt requested.
